author: Bastions
text: CORRECTION to this ball's description: bl-2bf7 WAS created and delivered (main commit 6b2edfb, 'lifecycle: state-branch sync on review and close [bl-2bf7]'). Its actual scope was the GIT-REMOTE participant only: require_remote_on_review/_on_close, bl review/close --sync/--no-sync, and rollback of the local state-branch commits on a required git-remote push failure. It did NOT touch the plugin dispatch path. So this ball (bl-fb4d) is still valid and needed, but it is NOT 'bl-2bf7 which never existed' — it is the PLUGIN-participant remainder the SPEC's §12/§14 prose lumped under bl-2bf7 but bl-2bf7's delivery did not cover: command-level consumption of plugin dispatch_push results (rollback/non-zero on a required plugin failure or Reject), EventCtx task_before/commit threading, and the §11 plugin override log + sync_status. Verified on main: cmd_review/close/update/create still do 'let _ = plugin::dispatch_push(...)'; no sync_status in src.

author: mark
text:
Enforce participant outcomes at the command layer

cmd_claim/review/close/update and cmd_create did 'let _ = plugin::dispatch_push(...)', computing a required participant's veto in the primitive but discarding it: the state-branch commit had landed and the command exited 0. They now consume the result via plugin::finish. On a required failure — a wire error or a first-class reject (SPEC 8.1) carrying plugin+reason — the event's state-branch commit is rolled back and the command exits non-zero with the reason verbatim (SPEC 9). claim rolls back by un-claiming (drop_worktree); the others hard-reset the state branch to a pre-event snapshot. The review squash on main and the close worktree teardown stay owned by their commands' control flow, deferred per the SPEC's staging as the ball scopes.

Best-effort skips record task.sync_status.<participant> (new Task field) with the verbatim reason, cleared on next success. Legacy-shim skips stay silent so SPEC 12 byte-identity holds for unmodified configs; only native participants populate sync_status. Gating is inert until bl-a46d.

EventCtx threads the SPEC 5.1 additive keys: pre-image task_before (absent on create), the event's state-branch commit sha, and the applied override tokens, delivered to a wants_context native plugin on the describe-gated side channel. The native protocol negotiates on the command's in-hand post-image so a close (task file already archived) can still be vetoed.

SPEC 11 per-invocation overrides --skip=NAME / --required=NAME are wired into InvocationOverrides on every lifecycle command (alongside the git-remote --sync/--no-sync) and logged in the state-branch commit subject so a post-hoc audit shows which negotiations ran without their required participants.

Conformance (tests/plugin_participant_enforce.rs): required reject aborts bl update and bl close and rolls back; best-effort reject ships and records sync_status; --skip ships past a required veto and is logged; gating is inert; a context-aware plugin sees task_before and commit. 100% coverage, clippy clean, every source file under the 300-line cap.
